folio.label: 145v
folio.l: The more is he holden , desolat
If that a Prynce , vsetú hasardrye
In alle gouänaunce , and policye
He is , as by comune opiniou~
I holde the lasse , in reputaciou~
¢ Stilbon~ , that was a wys embassadour
Was sent to Corynthe , in ful gret honour
Fro lacedomye , to maken here alliance
And whan he cam , hi~ happed ¹ chaunce
That alle the gretteste , that were of that lond
Pleiyngö atte hasard , he hem fond
For whicú , as sone as it mygút be
He stal hi~ hom a geyn , to his contre
And seide , there wyl I nat lese my name
Ne wyl nat take on me , so gret defame
ñow for to allie , vn to noone hasardoures
Sendetú othere wyse , embassadoures
For by my trouthe , me were leuere dye
Than I ©ow shulde , to hasardoures allye
For ©e that ben , so glorious in honours
Shuln nat allye ©ow , witú hasardours
As by my wyl , ne as be myn tretee
This wys Philosophre , thus seide he
Loke eke , that to the kyngö Demetrius
The kyngö of Parthe , as the boke seitú vs
Sent´ hi~ a peyre of dys of gold , in skorn~
For he had vsed hasard , there biforn~
For whicú he held his glorie , or his renou~
At no value , or reputaciou~
Lordes moun fynde , othere manäe pleye
Honest I now , to dryue the day a weye
¢ Now wyl I speke , of othes false and grete
A word or two , as olde bokes trete
Gret sweryngö is a thyngö , abhomynable
And false sweryngö is ©et , more repreuable
The heigú god , for bad sweryngö at al
Wytnesse on Mathew , but in special
Of sweryngö seitú , the holy Ieremye
Thow shalt swere sotú thyne othes , & nat lye
And swere in doom , and eke in rigútwysnesse
But Idel sweryngö , is a cursednesse
Byholde and se , that in the first table
Of heigú goddes hestes , honourable
How that the secund hest of hi~ , is this
Take nat myn name in Ydel , or amys
